title: Okta continues European expansion with creation of up to 200 jobs in new Dublin
summary: Okta, a leading identity provider, is opening a new office in Dublin's Docklands, aiming to create 200 new jobs by 2024. This expansion is part of Okta's EMEA growth strategy and will establish Dublin as an international hub, initially focusing on sales and marketing roles, with plans to expand into R&D and other critical business functions. The investment is supported by IDA Ireland and highlights Ireland's attractive talent pool for technology companies.

title: Okta continues European expansion with creation of up to 200 jobs in new Dublin office
summary: Okta, Inc. announced the opening of a new office in Dublin’s Docklands, Ireland, with plans to create 200 new jobs by 2024. This expansion is part of Okta’s EMEA growth strategy and will serve as an international hub, initially focusing on sales and marketing roles before expanding into R&D and critical business functions. The investment is supported by IDA Ireland and highlights Dublin's strong talent pool and evolving technology market.

title: Formerly Sacred Now Rotting Silicon Valley Dictum of “Growth at All Costs” Crushes Okta: Shares Collapse 80% from Peak
summary: Okta, a company that provides secure access to cloud-based systems, has seen its shares collapse by 80% from their February 2021 peak after reporting a $210 million net loss and lowering its guidance. Despite consistent and growing annual net losses exceeding $2 billion, executives and analysts have ironically reframed these as "profitability," highlighting a prevailing Silicon Valley mantra of "growth at all costs." The company's struggles are attributed to "sales challenges" and "integration challenges" related to its 2021 acquisition of Auth0, signaling the potential end of an era for this business model.

title: Okta’s Longtime Legal Chief Retiring After Layoffs, Lawsuit
summary: Jonathan Runyan, Okta Inc.'s general counsel, is retiring after eight years with the company, effective Friday, March 3rd, but will continue as an adviser until September 15. His departure comes as the authentication software company recently laid off 300 employees and is facing a shareholder lawsuit. Larissa Schwartz, a deputy general counsel, will succeed Runyan as the new top lawyer and corporate secretary.

title: Okta Introduces Okta AI – Securely Connecting People to Technology in the AI Era
summary: Okta, Inc. has announced Okta AI, a new suite of AI-powered capabilities designed to enhance security, user experience, and app development within its Workforce Identity Cloud and Customer Identity Cloud. This initiative leverages Okta's unique threat intelligence and Identity data, in collaboration with Google Cloud's Vertex AI, to accelerate AI innovation for Identity. Okta AI aims to provide real-time Identity actions while maintaining strict privacy and compliance standards for customer data.

title: Okta Settles Securities Suit Over Merger Fallout for $60 Million
summary: Okta Inc. has agreed to a $60 million settlement in a securities class-action lawsuit. Investors claimed the software company misrepresented its integration of an acquired company, leading to a significant stock drop. The settlement, which includes an unusual grant of class status by Okta, now awaits preliminary approval from a federal court.

title: Okta Securities Litigation settles for $60,000,000
summary: Eligible investors who acquired Okta Inc. Class A common stock between March 3, 2022, and August 31, 2022, can claim a portion of a $60,000,000 class action settlement. The lawsuit stemmed from alleged misstatements and omissions regarding the integration of Auth0 and a data security incident. The deadline to submit a claim with required proof of transaction is October 29, 2024.

title: Okta Executives Sell Shares in Recent Transactions
summary: Okta executives Jacques Frederic Kerrest (Director) and Brett Tighe (CFO) recently sold shares of Okta Class A Common Stock. Kerrest sold 3,306 shares for $293,733, leaving him with no direct ownership, while Tighe sold 5,000 shares for $445,023, retaining significant direct and indirect holdings. These transactions, reported in SEC Form 4 filings, both occurred on January 21, 2025.

title: US tech sector now decently undervalued
summary: The US technology sector, as of March 26, is now considered decently undervalued, shifting from being modestly overvalued a quarter ago. The median tech stock is 13% undervalued on a price-to-fair-value basis, with hardware, software, and semiconductors also below fair value estimates. Wide-moat names like Microsoft, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and Tyler Technologies, along with cybersecurity firms like Palo Alto Networks and Okta, are highlighted as attractive long-term investments due to their recurring revenue models and sticky customer bases.

title: 2 cybersecurity stocks well-positioned amid increased ransomware attacks
summary: Ransomware attacks are evolving, with cybercriminals using "double extortion" tactics and organized networks. This escalation is leading organizations to increase their IT security spending. Morningstar senior equity analyst Mark Cash identifies Okta (OKTA) and Palo Alto Networks (PANW) as two cybersecurity stocks well-positioned to benefit from this trend, driven by their innovative solutions for identity access, network security, and cloud protection.

title: Okta Announces Startup Challenge to Build New Identity Security Applications
summary: Okta has announced its 2nd annual Startup Challenge, inviting early-stage U.S. startups to build innovative identity, privacy, and security-enabled applications. Three finalists will pitch at Oktane25, with the winner potentially receiving a $500,000 investment from Okta, access to experts, and the opportunity to ring the Nasdaq opening bell. The challenge aims to foster innovation in identity management using Okta's platform, enabling startups to focus on product development without identity infrastructure complexities.

title: Okta Announces First Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Financial Results

title: Okta, Inc. Announces Settlement of Derivative Lawsuit and Adoption of Corporate Governance Reforms

Stockholders as of June 26, 2025, are affected and have the right to object to the settlement.

title: New Okta Innovations Secure the AI-Driven Enterprise and Combat Fraud with an Identity Security Fabric
summary: Okta has announced new capabilities for its Okta Platform and Auth0 Platform, designed to secure AI agents and combat AI-powered fraud through an identity security fabric. These innovations allow organizations to build secure AI agents with end-to-end lifecycle management and issue tamper-proof digital credentials to establish trust. Key features include Okta for AI Agents, Cross App Access (XAA) for secure agent and app interactions, and Verifiable Digital Credentials (VDC) to prevent fraud.

title: "AI security is identity security" - how Okta is weaving agents into the security fabric
summary: Okta, with Auth0, is addressing the significant security deficit caused by the widespread adoption of AI agents by introducing new security principles at its Oktane 2025 event. The company aims to seamlessly integrate AI agents into identity security frameworks through features like "Okta for AI Agents" for managing non-human identities, open industry-leading standards like Cross App Access (XAA) to secure app-to-app interactions, and future Verifiable Digital Credentials (VDC) to combat AI fraud and establish trust. These initiatives are designed to help organizations leverage AI's productivity gains without fear of exposure, providing centralized control, visibility, and governance over AI agent activities.
